name: Amy Allen
shortBio: Amy Allen is a solo singer-songwriter known for her soulful blend of Mississippi blues and folk traditions. Her music often reflects the rich cultural tapestry of her Southern roots.

name: Mechanical Bull
shortBio: Mechanical Bull is a dynamic four-piece indie rock band from Memphis, known for their energetic live performances and catchy, guitar-driven anthems. Their sound captures the raw spirit of the Bluff City.

name: Arrie Aslin
shortBio: Arrie Aslin is an artist-in-residence exploring the intersections of traditional folk and experimental soundscapes. Hailing from Natchez, her work pushes boundaries while staying grounded in Southern storytelling.

name: PLACEHOLDER: Mississippi Blues Artist A
shortBio: This artist is a compelling voice in the Mississippi corridor blues scene, carrying forward the rich traditions of the Delta. Their performances are steeped in the raw emotion and history of the genre.

name: PLACEHOLDER: Hudson Valley Americana Artist B
shortBio: Hailing from the scenic Hudson Valley, this artist crafts heartfelt Americana music that blends folk storytelling with roots-rock sensibilities. Their songs paint vivid pictures of life in upstate New York.

name: PLACEHOLDER: NOLA Jazz Artist C
shortBio: This New Orleans-based artist delivers an authentic taste of NOLA jazz, infusing traditional sounds with their unique contemporary flair. Their music embodies the vibrant spirit of the Crescent City.

name: PLACEHOLDER: Platinum Tier Artist D
shortBio: An established artist within our platinum tier, this musician is known for their significant contributions and high-quality artistic output. Based in Nashville, they represent a pinnacle of musical artistry.

name: PLACEHOLDER: Corridor Singer-Songwriter E
shortBio: This singer-songwriter, based in Vicksburg, offers poignant narratives and melodic tunes often inspired by the Mississippi corridor. Their work is a heartfelt reflection of their Southern surroundings.
